rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,2-diacyloxylation of styrenes with carboxylic acids to give vicinal diesters in acetone with nBu4NOAc electrolyte
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation generates carboxyl/styrene-derived radical species; carboxylate is oxidized at the anode and the styrene is converted via radical addition/oxidation to a benzylic cation that is trapped by carboxylate

key_intermediates.name: styrene-derived benzylic radical/cation after carboxyl radical addition
acyloxy radical from anodic oxidation of carboxylate
hydroxy ester by-product (32, 33) from water/hydroxide trapping

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the carboxylate (1b) to an acyloxy radical that adds to the styrene (1a) giving a benzylic radical; further anodic oxidation yields a benzylic cation trapped by a second carboxylate to deliver the 1,2-diester, with cathodic H2 evolution balancing charge.
notes: Page shows solvent/co-solvent screen (acetone best, 72%; MIBK, cyclopentanone, diethyl carbonate alone fail due to voltage overload or no reaction; aqueous co-solvent suppresses yield, consistent with hydroxy ester by-products 32 and 33). Mechanistic section indicates EPR experiments were performed but spectra/details are not on this page.
